name: Eight Bells Inn
description: 14th-century coaching inn with flagstone floors, real ales, and hearty pub food. Walkers warmly welcomed.

name: Badgers Hall Tearoom
description: Traditional Cotswold tearoom in a 15th-century building. Famous for homemade scones and afternoon tea.

name: Campden Coffee Company
description: Speciality coffee and light bites. Perfect for a caffeine boost before hitting the trail.

name: The Volunteer Inn
description: Friendly village local with a garden. Good selection of ales and simple bar snacks.

name: Noel Arms Hotel
description: Historic coaching inn offering comfortable rooms and a restaurant. A favourite overnight stop for day one walkers.

name: Public Water Tap (High Street)
description: Free drinking water tap near the Market Hall. Fill your bottles before setting off.

name: Public Toilets (Back Ends)
description: Free public toilets off the High Street, near the car park.

name: Campden Home & Garden
description: Well-stocked village shop with snacks, drinks, and basic supplies for the trail.

name: Broadway Tower Café
description: Café at the base of Broadway Tower serving hot drinks, sandwiches, and cake. Outdoor seating with views.

name: Water Tap (Tower Car Park)
description: Outdoor tap near the car park ticket machine. Seasonal — may be turned off in winter.

name: Toilets (Broadway Tower)
description: Public toilets at the Broadway Tower Country Park car park.

name: The Crown & Trumpet
description: Traditional village pub with real ales, home-cooked food, and B&B rooms. Dog-friendly and boot-friendly.

name: Tisanes Tea Rooms
description: Charming tearoom in a half-timbered cottage. Renowned cream teas and light lunches.

name: Broadway Deli
description: Artisan deli with local cheeses, charcuterie, bread, and trail-ready picnic supplies.

name: Russell's Restaurant
description: Award-winning restaurant in a former furniture factory. Great for a celebratory meal after day one.

name: Public Toilets (Church Street)
description: Free public toilets behind the church on Church Street.

name: Water Fountain (Village Green)
description: Drinking water fountain on the village green near the war memorial.

name: The Mount Inn
description: Cosy hilltop pub in Stanton with stunning views. Worth the short detour for a proper Cotswold pint.

name: Water Tap (Stanway Churchyard)
description: Outdoor tap in the churchyard. Refill your bottles in this peaceful spot.

name: The White Hart
description: 16th-century inn on the High Street with stone-flagged bar, real ales, and filling pub grub.

name: Juri's Tearoom
description: Family-run tearoom on North Street with homemade cakes and light lunches. A Winchcombe institution.

name: Wesley House Restaurant
description: Fine dining in a medieval half-timbered building. Excellent for a treat after a long day.

name: Winchcombe Camping
description: Small, friendly campsite on the edge of town. Hot showers, basic facilities, and lovely views.

name: Winchcombe Village Shop
description: Well-stocked village shop with fresh bread, snacks, drinks, and basic camping supplies.

name: Public Toilets (Back Lane)
description: Free public toilets near the town centre car park.

name: Water Tap (Recreation Ground)
description: Outdoor drinking water tap at the recreation ground on Abbey Terrace.

name: Rising Sun Hotel
description: Hilltop hotel with bar, restaurant, and rooms. Panoramic views from the terrace — the perfect summit reward.

name: Water Tap (Golf Club Car Park)
description: Outdoor tap in the golf club car park. Available during daylight hours.

name: The Exmouth Arms
description: Popular Cheltenham pub at the foot of Leckhampton Hill. Good beer garden and food.

name: Water Tap (Daisy Bank Car Park)
description: Outdoor tap at the Leckhampton Hill car park. Fill up before heading south.

name: Portable Toilets (Daisy Bank)
description: Portable facilities at the car park. Basic but functional.

name: The Falcon Inn
description: 16th-century coaching inn with flagstone floors. Real ales, seasonal food, and comfortable rooms.

name: St Michael's Restaurant
description: Relaxed bistro-style restaurant in a converted church. Excellent local seasonal menu.

name: Olivas Delicatessen
description: Artisan deli with local cheeses, cured meats, fresh bread, and trail snacks.

name: Public Toilets (New Street)
description: Free public toilets on New Street, near the church.

name: The Black Horse
description: Traditional Cotswold pub in Cranham village, a short detour from the trail. Good food and ales.

name: Prinknash Abbey Café
description: Peaceful café at the Benedictine abbey. Homemade cakes and coffee in tranquil gardens.

name: The Old Crown
description: Award-winning micropub in Uley village. Craft ales and ciders in a tiny but characterful space.

name: Uley Village Stores
description: Small village shop with essential supplies, snacks, and cold drinks.

name: Water Point (Coaley Peak Picnic Area)
description: Seasonal outdoor tap at the Coaley Peak picnic area.

name: The Old Spot Inn
description: CAMRA award-winning pub with excellent ales and locally sourced food. Named after the Gloucestershire Old Spots pig.

name: Kingshill House Café
description: Community arts centre café with fresh cakes, coffee, and light lunches in a beautiful setting.

name: Tesco Express
description: Convenience store for quick resupply — snacks, sandwiches, drinks, and basic supplies.

name: Public Toilets (Market Place)
description: Free public toilets in Dursley town centre.

name: Water Fountain (Parsonage Street)
description: Drinking water fountain near the library.

name: The Star Inn
description: Traditional town pub with real ales, bar food, and a warm welcome for muddy walkers.

name: Wotton Coffee Shop
description: Friendly independent café serving speciality coffee, homemade cakes, and toasted sandwiches.

name: Wotton Farm Shop
description: Local farm shop with fresh produce, bread, pies, and picnic supplies.

name: Public Toilets (Chipping Car Park)
description: Free public facilities at the main car park.

name: The Dog Inn
description: Welcoming village pub with hearty meals, local ales, and rooms. Popular with Cotswold Way walkers.

name: The Cross Hands
description: Country pub in nearby Tormarton with bar meals and accommodation. A handy stop before the final push.

name: The White Hart
description: Small village pub in Cold Ashton. Limited hours but a welcome sight on the final ridge.

name: Pennsylvania Farm Campsite
description: Basic but scenic farm camping near the trail. Hot showers and fire pits available.

name: The Salamander
description: Bath pub with craft beers and stonebaked pizzas. A great spot to toast completing the Cotswold Way.

name: Landrace Bakery
description: Artisan bakery and café near the Abbey. Sourdough, pastries, and excellent coffee.

name: Good Day Café
description: Bright, friendly café serving brunch, lunch, and speciality coffee. Perfect post-trail refuel.

name: Bath YHA
description: Budget-friendly hostel in a beautiful Italianate mansion. Dorms and private rooms available.

name: Water Fountain (Abbey Churchyard)
description: Public drinking water fountain right at the trail's end point.

name: Public Toilets (Bog Island)
description: Free public toilets near Pulteney Bridge, a short walk from the Abbey.

name: Water Tap (Stanway Cricket Ground)
description: Outdoor tap at the cricket pavilion. Seasonal — check before relying on it.

name: Stanway Tea Room
description: Small tea room in a converted barn near Stanway House. Cream teas and light lunches.

name: Water Point (Cleeve Common Car Park)
description: Seasonal outdoor tap at the main car park near the golf club.

name: The Rising Sun
description: Hilltop pub with spectacular views. Real ales and hearty home-cooked food.

name: Stream Crossing (Standish Wood)
description: Natural stream crossing on the trail. Not potable — use a filter or treat before drinking.

name: Old Sodbury Village Shop
description: Small community shop with snacks, cold drinks, and basic walking supplies.

name: Water Tap (St John's Church)
description: Outdoor tap in the churchyard. Please use respectfully.

name: Water Tap (Holy Trinity Church)
description: Small outdoor tap at the church gate. Seasonal — may be turned off in winter.

name: Cotswold House Hotel
description: Luxury boutique hotel on Chipping Campden's High Street. Spa, fine dining, and garden suites. Trail walker packages available.

name: Chance Cottage B&B
description: Traditional Cotswold stone B&B with comfortable rooms and a generous breakfast. Drying room for wet gear.

name: The Broadway Hotel
description: 16th-century coaching inn with modern comforts. Central location on the village green with a well-regarded restaurant.

name: Crown & Trumpet Inn
description: 17th-century inn with real ale bar and comfortable rooms. Walkers especially welcome — boot cleaning station outside.

name: Winchcombe Camping
description: Small, friendly campsite on the edge of town. Hot showers, fire pits, and a 5-minute walk to pubs and shops.

name: Sudeley Hill Farm B&B
description: Working farm B&B with views of Sudeley Castle. Hearty farmhouse breakfast with local eggs and sausages.

name: The Royal Oak
description: Historic coaching inn in the heart of Painswick. Rooms above the pub with real ales and home-cooked food below.

name: The Old Bell Hotel
description: Historic coaching inn in the town centre. Comfortable rooms and a restaurant serving local produce.

name: The Swan Hotel
description: Family-run hotel on the high street with a restaurant and bar. Good base for the Dursley to Old Sodbury section.

name: Manor Farm B&B
description: Cotswold stone farmhouse with views over the final ridge. Warm welcome and packed lunches available for the last day.

name: Russell's Fish & Chips
description: Award-winning fish and chips on Broadway High Street. Eat in or take away — perfect post-walk refuel.

name: Tisanes Tea Room
description: Elegant tearoom in a half-timbered building. Homemade scones with clotted cream and local jam.

name: The Plaisterers Arms
description: Quiet village local in Winchcombe with real ales, cider, and a small beer garden. Less touristy than the High Street pubs.

name: The Bakers Arms
description: Friendly pub in Broad Campden with a pretty beer garden. Home-cooked food and Donnington ales.

name: The Air Balloon (site)
description: Former pub at the A417 junction — now demolished for road improvements. Historical landmark for walkers.

name: The Falcon Inn
description: 16th-century coaching inn with flagstone floors and an open fire. Real ales and classic pub food.

name: Public Toilets (High Street Car Park)
description: Free public facilities at the main car park near the Market Hall.

name: Public Toilets (Church Close)
description: Clean public facilities behind St Eadburgha's Church, a short walk from the village green.

name: Public Toilets (Back Lane)
description: Free public facilities behind the town hall, signposted from the High Street.

name: Portaloo (Coaley Peak Car Park)
description: Portable toilet at the picnic site car park. Basic but functional — may not always be stocked.

name: Public Toilets (Stamages Lane)
description: Well-maintained public facilities near the churchyard. Free to use.

name: Co-op Food
description: Convenience store for last-minute supplies before heading south. Sandwiches, snacks, and trail essentials.

name: Spar Broadway
description: Well-stocked convenience store on the High Street. Sandwiches, cold drinks, and basic first aid supplies.

name: Winchcombe Village Stores
description: Independent village shop with local produce, walking snacks, and a good selection of cold drinks.

name: Boots Pharmacy (Stall Street)
description: Pharmacy near the Abbey for blister plasters, painkillers, and first aid supplies. The trail walker's best friend.

name: Water Fountain (War Memorial Garden)
description: Drinking water fountain in the war memorial garden on the village green.

name: Water Tap (Recreation Ground)
description: Outdoor drinking water tap at the recreation ground on Abbey Terrace.

name: Water Tap (Leckhampton Hill Car Park)
description: Outdoor tap at the Daisy Bank Road car park. Fill up before crossing Cleeve Common.

name: Stinchcombe Hill Camping
description: Simple camping with views over the Severn Vale. Composting toilet and outdoor cooking area. Book ahead.

name: Tormarton Field Camping
description: Wild-style camping in a farmer's field near the trail. Basic facilities but a beautiful spot for the last night.

name: Fish Hill Picnic Site Toilets
description: Public toilets at the Fish Hill picnic area on the A44, directly on the Cotswold Way.

name: Morris & Brown Café
description: Popular café on Broadway High Street serving brunch, coffee, and homemade cakes.

name: The Snowshill Arms
description: Village pub in nearby Snowshill serving real ales and hot food. About 1 mile off trail.

name: The Vine B&B
description: B&B in the picturesque village of Stanton, directly on the Cotswold Way.

name: Hayles Fruit Farm Campsite
description: Family-run farm campsite directly on the Cotswold Way near Hailes Abbey. Showers, toilets, fresh water.

name: Hayles Fruit Farm Café
description: Farm café serving teas, coffees, cakes and light lunches. Has public toilets and water refill. Right on the trail.

name: Postlip Hall Farm B&B
description: Working family farm B&B near Cleeve Common. En-suite rooms. Just off the Cotswold Way.

name: Co-op Food Winchcombe
description: Well-stocked supermarket for trail supplies in the centre of Winchcombe.

name: Williams of Winchcombe
description: Excellent deli selling fresh bread, cheese, charcuterie and picnic supplies.

name: Cotswold Way Café
description: Café on the Cotswold Way at Cleeve Hill Golf Course. Panoramic valley views at over 1,000ft.

name: Cleeve Hill Hotel
description: Four-star country hotel on the B4632 at the highest point of the Cotswolds. Restaurant and bar.

name: Colgate Farm Campsite
description: Simple farm campsite 200m off the Cotswold Way near Dowdeswell Woods. No booking needed.

name: Crickley Hill Country Park Café
description: Café at Crickley Hill Country Park serving snacks and hot drinks. Public toilets on site.

name: Public Toilets (Crickley Hill)
description: Free public toilets at Crickley Hill Country Park visitor centre. On the Cotswold Way.

name: The Royal George Hotel
description: 18th-century pub with 34 bedrooms in Birdlip village. Traditional pub food, good value rooms.

name: Big Skies Glamping
description: Tent pitches in a wildflower meadow just south of Leckhampton Hill. Amenities block, fire pits.

name: Star Bistro
description: Café near Crickley Hill at National Star College. Breakfast, lunch and tea. Weekdays only.

name: The Painswick Hotel
description: Boutique hotel in a Palladian mansion in the heart of Painswick. Fine dining restaurant.

name: The Bell Inn, Selsley
description: 16th-century Cotswold inn with 2 AA Rosettes. Real ales from Uley brewery. Just off the trail on Selsley Common.

name: Co-op Food King's Stanley
description: Convenience supermarket in King's Stanley village for trail supplies.

name: The King's Head
description: Village pub in King's Stanley serving meals and real ales near the Stroudwater canal.

name: The Star Inn, Whiteshill
description: Dog-friendly pub with rooms. Home-cooked Greek cuisine and local ales.

name: The Rose & Crown, Nympsfield
description: Village pub near Coaley Peak via a short diversion. Pub food and real ales.

name: Public Toilets (Coaley Peak)
description: Portable toilet facilities at the Coaley Peak viewpoint car park, directly on the Cotswold Way.

name: Ashen Plains Campsite
description: Quiet woodland campsite about 1 mile off the Cotswold Way south of Dursley.

name: The Black Horse Inn
description: 16th-century village pub at the foot of Nibley Knoll. Indian and British food, B&B rooms.

name: Sainsbury's Dursley
description: Large supermarket just off the main street in Dursley for trail supplies.

name: Co-op Food Wotton-under-Edge
description: Supermarket in Wotton town centre. Stock up here — facilities are sparse until Old Sodbury.

name: Public Toilets (Wotton Rope Walk)
description: Free public toilets on The Rope Walk in Wotton-under-Edge. Last public toilets before Old Sodbury.

name: The Fox Inn, Hawkesbury Upton
description: Village pub in Hawkesbury Upton. One of the few refreshment stops between Wotton and Old Sodbury.

name: The Beaufort Arms
description: Traditional village pub in Hawkesbury Upton. Short detour from the trail.

name: Hawkesbury Stores
description: Village shop and post office. Critical resupply point between Wotton and Old Sodbury.

name: The Compass Inn
description: Village pub in Tormarton between Old Sodbury and Cold Ashton. Accommodation available.

name: Pennsylvania Garage Shop
description: Petrol station shop on the A46. Hot and cold food, drinks, and snacks.

name: Toghill House Farm B&B
description: Five-room B&B about 1 mile off the Cotswold Way near Cold Ashton.

name: Hill Farm B&B
description: Two-room B&B directly on the Cotswold Way at Cold Ashton.

name: Tesco Express Weston
description: Convenience store in Weston on the outskirts of Bath. First shop since Cold Ashton area.

name: Parsons Bakery, Weston
description: Bakery and café on Weston High Street. Fresh pastries, sandwiches and hot drinks.

name: Public Toilets (Victoria Park)
description: Public toilets in Royal Victoria Park, Bath. On the trail route approaching Bath Abbey.

name: The Raven
description: Historic pub near Bath Abbey. Award-winning pies and real ales. Perfect for celebrating completing the trail.

name: Bath Backpackers Hostel
description: Budget hostel in central Bath. Dorm beds and private rooms.
